channel.title: La Cyber Sphère — Bulletin de veille cyber
channel.description: Alertes critiques et élevées, résumées et sourcées.

item.title: [Faible] Microsoft working to fix Exchange Online mailbox quarantine issue
item.description: Microsoft is working to resolve an ongoing Exchange Online issue that has been mistakenly quarantining customers' mailboxes since Sunday. [...]

item.title: [Critique] Check Point warns of SmartConsole zero-day exploited in attacks
item.description: Israeli cybersecurity firm Check Point Software has addressed an actively exploited zero-day flaw in the company's SmartConsole graphical user interface (GUI) admin panel. [...]

item.title: [Moyen] Check Point Patches Exploited SmartConsole Flaw Allowing Full Admin Access
item.description: Check Point has released security updates to address multiple vulnerabilities impacting Security Management and Multi-Domain Management (MDSM) products, including a critical flaw that has come under active exploitation…

item.title: [Critique] Swiss train maker Stadler refuses Everest $12 million ransomware demand
item.description: Stadler Rail said it will not make a $12.3 million ransom payment after cybercriminals stole technical data from a supplier's file-sharing platform.

item.title: [Faible] Upbound says hack caused $13 million in fraudulent Acima leases
item.description: The Upbound Group fintech company disclosed that threat actors who stole data from its systems leveraged it to create $13 million in Acima leases. [...]

item.title: [Faible] Extension of CISA 2015 info-sharing protections passes as part of House’s defense bill
item.description: A 10-year renewal of the cybersecurity information-sharing law known as CISA 2015 passed as part of the House's fiscal 2027 defense authorization bill.

item.title: [Élevé] South Korea discloses data breach impacting diplomats worldwide
item.description: South Korea disclosed that hackers breached the National Diplomatic Academy's online education system for ten months and stole personal information belonging to current and former employees of the Ministry of Foreign…

item.title: [Faible] Federal agencies broaden alert on Iran-linked OT attacks
item.description: The observed incidents include “malicious project file interactions and manipulation of data on human machine interface (HMI) and supervisory control and data acquisition (SCADA) displays,” the advisory says.

item.title: [Faible] GitHub Cuts Public Bug Bounty Payouts, Moves Top Rewards to VIP Tier
item.description: Beginning July 27, 2026, GitHub will cut public bug bounty payouts by at least half at every severity level. Critical findings will drop from $20,000-$30,000+ to a fixed $10,000, while its permanent invite-only VIP tier…

item.title: [Moyen] Ubuntu snap-confine Flaw Could Give Local Users Root on Default Desktop Installs
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new local privilege escalation (LPE) vulnerability in snap-confine that an unprivileged user can trigger to obtain root access and gain complete control of a target…

item.title: [Faible] French Parliament greenlights social media ban for under-15s
item.description: Both houses of the French Parliament voted to block social media access for children under 15, making France the first European country to enact a ban amid a broadening global crackdown.

item.title: [Faible] Une IA d’OpenAI s’échappe de son environnement de test et pirate Hugging Face
item.description: Un agent autonome propulsé par des modèles avancés d’OpenAI aurait échappé à son environnement de test avant de... L’article Une IA d’OpenAI s’échappe de son environnement de test et pirate Hugging Face est apparu en…

item.title: [Élevé] BANATIC : plus de 91 000 élus et responsables de collectivités exposés après une cyberattaque
item.description: Le hacker misere a revendiqué, le 20 juillet 2026, la compromission de banatic.interieur.gouv.fr, une plateforme du ministère de... L’article BANATIC : plus de 91 000 élus et responsables de collectivités exposés après…

item.title: [Critique] Swiss rail giant Stadler rejects $12.3M ransom demand after cyberattack
item.description: Swiss rail vehicle manufacturer Stadler Rail says the Everest ransomware gang demanded about $12.3 million after breaching a data exchange platform shared with one of its suppliers. [...]

item.title: [Faible] Rassemblement National : une base WordPress de 95 tables en fuite après une cyberattaque
item.description: Le site officiel du Rassemblement National fait l’objet d’une revendication publiée le 20 juillet 2026. Un acteur affirme... L’article Rassemblement National : une base WordPress de 95 tables en fuite après…

item.title: [Élevé] New Kimsuky campaign compromised South Korean software vendors
item.description: A North Korean advanced persistent threat (APT) group recently targeted vendors of collaborative-work software, South Korean researchers said.

item.title: [Faible] Japanese food logistics giant recovers as extortion group claims cyberattack
item.description: Nichirei Logistics Group said warehouse operations and frozen food shipments are returning to normal. A cybercrime gang said it caused the disruption.

item.title: [Critique] How enterprise GenAI can amplify ransomware risk — and how to contain it
item.description: Enterprise AI can accelerate ransomware attacks when AI assistants and agents inherit excessive permissions or compromised identities. Acronis explains how identity controls, governance, and least-privilege access help…

item.title: [Moyen] Adobe Acrobat Extension Flaw Let Malicious Sites Read WhatsApp Web Data
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a now-patched vulnerability chain in the Adobe Acrobat Chrome extension that has over 314 million users, which, if exploited, could facilitate a silent hijack of a…

item.title: [Moyen] New InfraTrust report reveals infrastructure flaws admins should patch first
item.description: Eclypsium has launched InfraTrust, a new infrastructure cybersecurity knowledge base and monthly InfraTrust Pulse report designed to help organizations prioritize vulnerabilities affecting infrastructure, firmware,…

item.title: [Faible] Adobe Chrome extension flaw let sites access private WhatsApp chats
item.description: The Adobe Acrobat extension for Chrome could be used to access conversations and data rendered in WhatsApp Web without any form of authentication. [...]

item.title: [Critique] Hackers Exploit Windmill Flaw to Read Arbitrary Server Files Without Authentication
item.description:
A high-severity security flaw impacting open-source developer platform Windmill has come under active exploitation in the wild, per VulnCheck.
The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-29059 (CVSS score: 7.5), a case…

item.title: [Faible] OpenAI models behind breach of Hugging Face systems, companies say
item.description: OpenAI announced that its models were behind a breach of the AI platform Hugging Face, which had earlier detected an attack carried out by "by an autonomous AI agent."

item.title: [Critique] The Fastest Path to AI Adoption Runs Through Security
item.description: Security leaders who build fast, visible paths to AI adoption are becoming the most valued partners in their organizations. AI governance done right gives security teams the visibility they need, employees the tools…

item.title: [Critique] CISA orders urgent action on actively exploited Langflow RCE flaw
item.description: The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday ordered U.S. government agencies to prioritize patching an actively exploited vulnerability in the Langflow visual framework for building AI agents.…

item.title: [Faible] Why Modern SOCs Need Multi-Layered Detections
item.description: The cycle is over. For years, cybersecurity followed a familiar pattern: defenses improved, attackers adapted, and the back-and-forth continued. Today, AI-equipped attackers are simply outpacing defenses. Most…

item.title: [Faible] Stop renting storage space — this lifetime 2TB plan is yours for $59
item.description: Cloud storage costs tend to creep up over time, since most services charge monthly or annually for as long as you use them. FileJump's Lifetime Plan skips that model entirely, offering 2TB of cloud storage for a single…

item.title: [Faible] Microsoft to stop Exchange 2016 / 2019 security updates in October
item.description: Microsoft has reminded customers that it will stop shipping security updates for Exchange 2016 and 2019 through the Extended Security Update (ESU) program in October. [...]
